message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Pathogenic coronaviruses represent a major threat to global public health. Here, using a recombinant reporter virus-based compound screening approach, we identified several small-molecule inhibitors that potently block the replication of the newly emerged severe acute respiratory syndrome virus 2 (SARS-CoV-2). Among them, JIB-04 inhibited SARS-CoV-2 replication in Vero E6 cells with an EC
                  <jats:sub>50</jats:sub>
                  of 695 nM, with a specificity index of greater than 1,000. JIB-04 showed
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  antiviral activity in multiple cell types against several DNA and RNA viruses, including porcine coronavirus transmissible gastroenteritis virus. In an
                  <jats:italic>in vivo</jats:italic>
                  porcine model of coronavirus infection, administration of JIB-04 reduced virus infection and associated tissue pathology, which resulted in improved weight gain and survival. These results highlight the potential utility of JIB-04 as an antiviral agent against SARS-CoV-2 and other viral pathogens.
                </jats:p>
title: JIB-04 has broad-spectrum antiviral activity and inhibits SARS-CoV-2 replication and coronavirus pathogenesis
